title: Quantum Computation and Quantum Information (Nielsen & Chuang)
subtitle: The standard textbook on quantum computing — qubits, gates, algorithms, error correction, and quantum information theory

summary.one_line: The definitive textbook on quantum computation and quantum information, covering the mathematical foundations, quantum algorithms (Shor, Grover), quantum error correction, entanglement theory, and ...
summary.scope: Covers quantum mechanics for computing (qubits, density operators, measurements), quantum circuits and gates, quantum algorithms (Deutsch-Jozsa, Shor's factoring, Grover's search), quantum error correction, quantum information theory (entropy, Holevo bound, no-cloning), entanglement theory, and physical implementations (NMR, ion traps, superconducting qubits).

sources.label: Michael A. Nielsen & Isaac L. Chuang — Quantum Computation and Quantum Information: 10th Anniversary Edition (Cambridge University Press, 2010). PDF, 7.5 MB.

body.text: The qubit is the minimal unit of quantum information — a superposition of |0> and |1>. This IS the (S0, 0, 1) structure from the Tractatus: S0 is the qubit's state space (the Bloch sphere), |0> and |1> are the poles, and measurement forces the qubit into one pole or the other. The quantum computer operates in the superposition layer — it computes with uncommitted S0, not with collapsed configurations. Quantum error correction is the technology of maintaining S0 against decoherence. This maps directly to the constraint space: a quantum computer is a device that navigates C at L0 rather than L3.
